channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Ghatak UCAV vs Okhotnik vs X-47B: How India’s Stealth Drone Stacks Up
item.description: India’s upcoming Ghatak UCAV is often compared with Russia’s S-70 Okhotnik and the US Navy’s X-47B, both of which represent advanced flying-wing stealth unmanned combat platforms. While all three share a similar design philosophy focused on low observability and autonomous strike capability, their scale, maturity, and intended roles show key differences.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Iran’s Last Flyable C-130 Hercules Transport Aircraft Evacuated to Pakistan Amid Fears of Israeli and U.S. Airstrikes
item.description: In a desperate bid to safeguard its remaining military airlift capability, the Islamic Republic of Iran Air Force (IRIAF) has flown its final two operational C-130E and C-130H Hercules transport aircraft to Pakistan. The move comes as Iranian commanders scramble to protect these aging but vital platforms from potential follow-on strikes by Israeli and American forces during the ongoing regional conflict.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Expanding permanent category with veto critical to UNSC reform: India
item.description: India has asserted that any reform of the United Nations Security Council not accompanied by expansion in the permanent category with veto would perpetuate existing imbalance and inequities in the UN organ.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Another steam generator dispatched to NPCIL ahead of schedule, says L&T
item.description: Infrastructure major Larsen & Toubro (L&T) on Wednesday said that its heavy engineering arm has dispatched another steam generator to the Nuclear Power Corporation of India Ltd (NPCIL) in an effort to fast-track the country’s energy security goal.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Sri Lanka repatriates over 230 Iranian sailors from two naval vessels
item.description: Over 230 sailors of two Iranian Naval vessels distressed in Sri Lanka after a US submarine attack and engine failure respectively have been repatriated, defence officials said here Wednesday. A total of 238 sailors were sent back on board a Turkish Airliner late on Tuesday night. On March 4, Iran’s naval ship ‘Iris Dena’ was attacked by the US killing 84 on board outside Sri Lanka’s international waters. As many as 32 others were rescued by Sri Lanka.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Iran used Chinese spy satellite to target US bases during conflict
item.description: Iran secretly acquired a Chinese spy satellite, giving the Islamic Republic a new capability to target US military bases across the West Asia during the recent war, the Financial Times reported on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Attacks On Merchant Shipping Completely Unacceptable: EAM Jaishankar At AZEC-Plus Meet
item.description: External Affairs Minister (EAM) S Jaishankar on Wednesday underlined India’s strong commitment to safe and unimpeded transit passage of maritime shipping while assuring that India will continue to work with like-minded partners to develop supply chain resilience.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Navy Chief Admiral Tripathi commends force’s role in safeguarding energy security amid West Asia conflict
item.description: India Navy chief Admiral Dinesh K. Tripathi has commended the force for its role in safeguarding India’s maritime interests, particularly in ensuring energy security amid the ongoing tensions in West Asia.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Marshal Arjan Singh’s legacy continues to inspire generations: IAF, Defence Ministry pay tribute on 107th anniversary
item.description: The Indian Air Force on Wednesday recalled the legacy of war hero Marshal Arjan Singh on his 107thbirth anniversary, describing him as a symbol of exceptional leadership, courage and foresight, whose service to the nation continues to inspire generations of air warriors.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Farooq Abdullah says Pakistan should realise conflict only brings destruction
item.description: National Conference (NC) president Farooq Abdullah on Wednesday said Pakistan should realise that nothing can be achieved through conflict, as it only brings destruction.Pakistan should put an end to conflict, Abdullah told reporters on the sidelines of an Army event at the Jammu and Kashmir Light Infantry (JAKLI) regiment at Rangreth here.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: INS Hansa SBTF to get Upgrades : India Prepares Shore-Based Test Facility for future Twin-Engine Deck Fighter Trials
item.description: India’s Shore Based Test Facility (SBTF) at INS Hansa, Goa—recognized as the only facility of its kind in Asia and among just three globally—is undergoing a series of targeted upgrades to prepare for trials of the Twin Engine Deck Based Fighter (TEDBF). As of early 2026, multiple tenders have been issued to recalibrate and modernize the facility, signaling a transition from Naval LCA testing to the more demanding TEDBF prototype phase.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: NewSpace Unveils Multi-Layered UGV Warfare Ecosystem with Vanguard, Airawat, Pioneer and Scout Platforms
item.description: NewSpace Research & Technologies Pvt Ltd has taken a significant step toward expanding India’s autonomous warfare capabilities by unveiling a new lineup of Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs), signalling its formal entry into the land systems domain. Rather than focusing on a single platform, the company is developing a family of interoperable UGVs designed to operate as part of a layered, network-centric combat architecture.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: AMCA Mk2 Engine Race Heats Up as Rolls-Royce and Safran Pitch Future-Proof Modular Designs
item.description: India’s high-stakes fighter engine decision for the AMCA Mk2 is evolving beyond a conventional procurement into a long-term strategic architecture debate, with Rolls-Royce and Safran now positioning their proposals around modular, upgradeable propulsion ecosystems rather than static engine designs.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: No India didn’t deploy INS Dhruv to track Pakistani Missile Test as claimed by Pro-Pakistani Website Report
item.description: A claim by a pro-Pakistani defence news website based in Malaysia alleging that India deployed its advanced missile tracking vessel INS Dhruv in response to a Pakistani NOTAM has come under scrutiny due to the absence of verifiable evidence. The report suggested that India reacted to a large exclusion zone—spanning roughly 415 to 450 kilometers off Pakistan’s coastline near Karachi, Ormara, Gwadar, and Sonmiani—by positioning INS Dhruv in the Arabian Sea to monitor a suspected missile test conducted between April 14 and 15, 2026.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Tejas Mk1 Shines at Tarang Shakti 2024 with Reported 4-0 Edge Over EA-18G Growler
item.description: India’s flagship indigenous fighter, the LCA Tejas Mk1, has emerged as a major talking point following Exercise Tarang Shakti 2024, with circulating reports suggesting that the aircraft achieved a notable edge in simulated engagements against the Royal Australian Air Force’s EA-18G Growler. Conducted in two phases, with the second phase held at Jodhpur Air Force Station from August 30 to September 14, the multinational exercise brought together some of the most capable air platforms in the world for complex Large Force Engagements (LFE).
item.dc:creator: admin
